about.title: About
about.description: Product identity, version metadata, and release entry points for this build.
about.hero.copy: Lingua keeps fast multi-language experiments inside one focused editor shell.
about.field.versionHint: Desktop reads the packaged app version; web falls back to bundled metadata.
about.field.buildDateHint: Approximate bundle timestamp for the current build.
about.links.label: Project links
about.links.hint: Open the canonical repository, website, or license document.
about.actions.label: Release actions
about.actions.hint: Jump into updates, release notes, or onboarding from the identity section.
whatsNew.title: What's New

tour.options.dontShowAgain: Don't show this tour on startup
about.actions.showTourOnStartup.label: Show guided tour on startup
about.actions.showTourOnStartup.hint: When off, Lingua stops auto-starting the guided tour on launch. You can still replay it anytime from the button above.
tour.step.editor.title: Editor workspace
tour.step.editor.text: This is the main coding surface. Templates, inline results, and runtime markers all stay anchored to the editor so you can iterate without leaving the workspace.
tour.step.run.title: Run the active file
tour.step.run.text: Click Run to continue. The tour waits for a real execution here so you can see the feedback loop instead of skipping past it.
tour.step.console.title: Console feedback
tour.step.console.text: Logs, warnings, errors, and timing badges land here. When execution emits structured output, Lingua keeps the console and inline panel in sync.
tour.step.explorer.title: Project explorer
tour.step.explorer.text: Use the explorer to open folders, create files, and keep tabs tied to the working tree instead of isolated scratch files.
tour.step.toolbar.title: Toolbar controls
tour.step.toolbar.text: Quick-open, command palette, snippets, console visibility, and settings all live in the top toolbar so the core workflow stays one click away.
tour.step.snippets.title: Snippet library
tour.step.snippets.text: Save useful code from the active tab into reusable snippets, then reopen it later in a new tab or insert it into the current file.
tour.step.commandPalette.title: Command palette
tour.step.commandPalette.text: The command palette is the fastest way to navigate templates, snippets, layout changes, and actions. Open it anytime with Cmd+Shift+P or Ctrl+Shift+P.
appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Lingua supports a dark-first shell and a refined light mode without changing your editor runtime settings.

appearance.theme.light.label: Light

editor.title: Editor
editor.description: Keep the Monaco surface aligned with how you read code and how aggressively you want automatic safeguards.

editor.restoreSession.label: Reopen last session

editor.formatOnSave.label: Format on save
editor.formatOnSave.hint: Run Prettier for JavaScript, TypeScript, JSON, and CSS, gofmt / rustfmt for Go and Rust, and ruff (falling back to black) for Python before writing the file.

editor.vimMode.hint: Enables Vim-style keybindings inside the editor. A localized status bar at the bottom of the editor shows the active mode. Macros via qa…q + @a are supported.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Lingua is local-first. Telemetry sends coarse usage signals; crash reports are opt-in and may include Electron diagnostic minidumps.
privacy.telemetry.label: Share anonymous usage analytics
privacy.telemetry.hint: Sends coarse signals (app version, OS bucket, which overlays you opened) to the Lingua team so we can spot regressions. Off by default.
privacy.telemetry.notSet: Not set — telemetry stays off until you opt in.
privacy.telemetry.granted: Enabled — thanks for helping improve Lingua.
privacy.telemetry.declined: Declined — no data leaves your machine.
privacy.firstRun.title: Help improve Lingua?
privacy.firstRun.body: Lingua can send anonymous usage signals (app version, OS bucket, which overlays you opened) so we can spot regressions. Your code, file paths, and project contents never leave your machine.
privacy.firstRun.changeLater: You can change this anytime in Settings → Privacy.
settings.account.runCapsules.title: Run capsules
settings.account.runCapsules.description: Export the latest run as a portable JSON capsule. The capsule includes the source code for replay; metadata and output are sanitized where applicable. Review the JSON before sharing it. Nothing leaves your machine unless you paste it yourself.

license.title: License
license.description: Paid tiers unlock via a signed license token issued at linguacode.dev. Offline-verifiable — no network call required.
license.current.label: Current plan
license.current.hint: The active tier is derived from your signed license, or Free if none is applied.
license.badge.tooltip: Current license tier: {{tier}}. Click to open License settings.
license.paste.label: Paste a license token
license.paste.hint: Paste the string from your Lingua account email. The value is signed; we verify it locally.
license.notice.activated: License activated — you are on the {{tier}} tier.
license.notice.invalid: Could not activate that license. Check the token and try again.
license.notice.invalid.malformed: That token isn't in the expected format. Copy the full string from your Lingua account email and try again.
license.notice.invalid.signature: The signature on that license does not match. The token may have been edited or truncated — paste the original string again.
license.notice.invalid.expired: That license is past its support window. Renew from your Lingua account to keep Pro features active.
license.notice.invalid.clockSkew: Your system clock is more than 24 hours off from the license's issue date. Correct the clock and try again.
license.notice.invalid.unsupportedTier: That license is for a tier this build of Lingua doesn't recognize. Update to the latest version and try again.
license.notice.invalid.notAccepted: This build of Lingua cannot accept licenses. Install an official Lingua release to activate your plan.
license.notice.invalid.devicesExhausted: This license already has the maximum number of web devices registered. Remove a device on another browser and try again.
license.notice.invalid.refunded: This license was refunded. Use a different one or contact support if this looks wrong.
license.notice.invalid.unknownLicense: We could not find this license on the server. Make sure you copied the full token from your Lingua account email.
license.notice.invalid.requestRejected: The license server rejected this device's registration. The token is valid but the request did not pass server checks. Update Lingua to the latest version, or contact support if the issue persists.
license.notice.serverUnreachable: Activated locally as {{tier}}. We could not reach the license server right now — Lingua will sync the device list the next time you are online.
license.notice.cleared: License removed. You are back on the Free plan.
license.notice.clearFailed: The license could not be removed. Check disk permissions and try again.

license.education.notice.confirmationSen: Confirmation email sent. Click the link in your inbox to receive your educational token.
license.education.notice.notEducational: That email is not on our educational allow-list. Use a school email or open a support ticket if your institution should be supported.

license.education.notice.unreachable: Could not reach the license server. Try again in a moment.
license.education.notice.disabled: This build of Lingua cannot register educational licenses. Install an official Lingua release.

license.recovery.notice.sent: If that email matches a Lingua license, we just resent the token. Check your inbox.
license.recovery.notice.invalidEmail: That email looks malformed. Double-check it and try again.

validation.success.json: JSON validation passed. No syntax issues found.
validation.success.yaml: YAML validation passed. No structural issues found.
validation.success.dotenv: .env validation passed. No malformed or duplicate entries found.
validation.success.csv: CSV validation passed. Column shapes are consistent.
validation.success.editorconfig: EditorConfig validation passed. Known keys and values only.
validation.success.dockerfile: Dockerfile validation passed. No common issues detected.
validation.success.gitignore: Gitignore validation passed. No suspicious patterns detected.
validation.success.makefile: Makefile validation passed. Recipe indentation looks consistent.
validation.success.shellscript: Shell script validation passed. Shebang and safety-mode flags look fine.
validation.dotenv.duplicateKey: Duplicate key "{{key}}" also defined on line {{previousLine}}.
validation.editorconfig.expectedKeyValue: Expected "key = value" or a section header like "[*]".
validation.editorconfig.indentSizeInvali: indent_size must be a positive integer or the literal "tab".
validation.editorconfig.invalidEnumValue: "{{value}}" is not a valid value for {{key}}. Expected one of: {{allowed}}.
validation.dockerfile.maintainerDeprecat: MAINTAINER is deprecated. Use LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="..." instead.
validation.dockerfile.firstMustBeFrom: The first instruction in a Dockerfile must be FROM (ARG is the only exception).
validation.dockerfile.preferRunOverAddUr: Prefer RUN with curl/wget + COPY over ADD <url>; ADD does not verify checksums.
validation.dockerfile.imageRefNoTag: "{{imageRef}}" has no tag — pin a specific tag (or digest) for reproducible builds.
validation.dockerfile.latestTagUnpinned: :latest tags are not pinned. Use a specific tag or a digest for reproducible builds.
validation.dockerfile.aptGetMissingFlags: apt-get install without -y will block the build. Add -y --no-install-recommends and clean up rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*.
validation.dockerfile.userRoot: USER root (uid 0) runs the final container as root. Prefer a dedicated non-root user for runtime images.
validation.dockerfile.missingFrom: Dockerfile is missing a FROM instruction.
validation.dockerfile.exposeWithoutHealt: EXPOSE advertises a port but no HEALTHCHECK is defined. Orchestrators (Compose, Kubernetes, Swarm) read HEALTHCHECK to decide whether traffic should land.
validation.gitignore.trailingWhitespace: Trailing whitespace changes the pattern. If you meant to match a literal space, escape it with a backslash; otherwise strip the trailing spaces.
validation.gitignore.backslashSeparator: gitignore patterns use forward slashes, not backslashes, even on Windows.
validation.gitignore.duplicatePattern: Duplicate pattern "{{pattern}}" also appears on line {{previous}}.
validation.makefile.spaceIndentedRecipe: Recipe for "{{target}}" is indented with spaces. Makefiles require a hard tab before each command.
validation.makefile.orphanTabCommand: Tab-indented command has no preceding target — Make will reject this.
validation.makefile.duplicateTarget: Target "{{name}}" is already defined on line {{previous}}.
validation.makefile.missingPhony: Target "{{target}}" is almost always virtual — add it to a .PHONY declaration so Make doesn't skip it when a file of the same name exists.
validation.makefile.unusedVariable: Variable "{{name}}" is assigned but never referenced. Remove it or use it via $({{name}}).
validation.shellscript.missingShebang: Shell script has no shebang. Add #!/usr/bin/env bash (or #!/bin/sh) as the first line so the file runs under a predictable interpreter.
validation.shellscript.missingSafetyMode: No safety-mode flags detected. Add set -euo pipefail near the top so the script aborts on the first failure instead of silently chaining errors.

recovery.safeMode.hint: Open the app with session restore and plugin discovery skipped. Use when a corrupted state blocks normal boot.
recovery.factoryNotice.title: Factory recovery active
recovery.factoryNotice.body: Lingua detected three crashes within a minute and started with risky restore paths skipped. Your license is intact. Reload normally once you finish recovering.
chrome.unsaved.label: unsaved

bottomPanel.tabs.variablesHint: Inspect captured variables from the last run · Shift+Cmd+I
shortcuts.item.toggleVariableInspectorSu: Switch the variable inspector between the floating card and the bottom panel tab.
variableInspector.surface.notice.toFloat: Variable inspector docked to the floating card.
variableInspector.surface.notice.toBotto: Variable inspector docked to the bottom panel.
